arts.ttl: We helped raise Premier League bar – Pep Guardiola responds to Capello criticism
arts.sum: Guardiola has won six Premier League titles and the Champions League with Manchester City.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola fires back at Capello’s jibe about making football boring
arts.sum: ‘It’s not the first time he’s said that’ Capello said Guardiola ruined Italian football Pep Guardiola has responded sarcastically to Fabio Capello’s claim that he is arrogant, has “ruined Italian football” and has made the sport boring. The former England coach offered his view of Guardiola to the Spanish newspaper El Mundo this week. On Friday Manchester City’s manager was asked whether he listened to someone of the Italian’s stature in the game. “I listen to everything that people say about me. Everything. So be careful. I am controlling you,” he said as a joke. “It’s not the first time that Mr Fabio Capello said that. I’m not good enough to win Italian football. Italian football is much, much more important than the way you do it. A big hug from Fabio. A big hug.” Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola praises ‘brilliant’ Atlético after UCL elimination at hands of Real Madrid
arts.sum: Manchester City head coach Pep Guardiola has moved to reveal his appreciation for the efforts of Diego Simeone’s Atlético Madrid, en route to being sent packing from the Champions League…

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola: Man City boss responds to scathing Fabio Capello attack
arts.sum: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola brushed aside Fabio Capello's scathing attack on his character and coaching career, instead offering a "big hug" to the spiky Italian pundit. "It's not the first time Mr Fabio Capello has said that," Guardiola continued.
